theory.concept:
CASE lets you add if/else logic directly inside a query, returning different values depending on a condition.

- Works like a series of if/else-if checks — conditions are evaluated in order, and the first one that's true wins
- Can be used inside SELECT (to create a computed column), inside ORDER BY, or inside WHERE/HAVING
- Always include an ELSE — without it, rows that match none of the conditions return NULL, which can be surprising if unintended
- Two forms exist: "simple CASE" (compares one column to fixed values) and "searched CASE" (each branch has its own full condition) — the searched form shown below is more flexible and more commonly used
theory.syntax:
SELECT column1,
  CASE
    WHEN condition1 THEN result1
    WHEN condition2 THEN result2
    ELSE default_result
  END AS alias_name
FROM table_name;

example.query:
-- 1. Categorizing marks into a grade band
SELECT name, marks,
  CASE
    WHEN marks >= 85 THEN 'A'
    WHEN marks >= 70 THEN 'B'
    ELSE 'C'
  END AS grade
FROM Students;

-- 2. Using CASE inside an aggregate to count conditionally
SELECT
  SUM(CASE WHEN city = 'Delhi' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS delhi_count
FROM Students;

shared_table.name: Students
shared_table.create_sql:
CREATE TABLE Students (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
  name TEXT,
  age INTEGER,
  city TEXT,
  marks INTEGER
);
shared_table.insert_sql:
INSERT INTO Students (id, name, age, city, marks) VALUES
(1, 'Aditya', 21, 'Kolkata', 78),
(2, 'Priya', 22, 'Delhi', 85),
(3, 'Rohan', 21, 'Kolkata', 62),
(4, 'Sneha', 23, 'Kolkata', 91),
(5, 'Karan', 21, 'Bangalore', 55),
(6, 'Anjali', 22, 'Delhi', 73),
(7, 'Vikram', 20, 'Chennai', 88),
(8, 'Meera', 22, 'Delhi', 67);

questions.prompt: Label each student as 'Pass' if marks are 60 or above, otherwise 'Fail'.
Label each student's age group as 'Young' (under 21), 'Mid' (21-22), or 'Senior' (above 22).
Count how many students fall into each grade band: 'A' (85+), 'B' (70-84), 'C' (below 70).
Select the names of students, sorted so that students from 'Kolkata' appear first, followed by everyone else alphabetically by name.
